title: Memory System Derived-Data GDPR Position

A.label: no_context
A.notes: Correct directional decision (cascade-delete, EDPB over Hamburg) but fabricates specifics: 24-month retention default, REST API endpoints, explicit deletion propagation order (leaf-first) that conflicts with ground truth CASCADE approach. Misses co-location simplification argument, redaction-at-insert for assistant_thinking, 19-78% leakage rate, --immediate flag, source JSONL handling. Cites Song & Raghunathan 2020 and Carlini 2021 (valid but not in ground truth). Missing 11 of 15 ground truth references. No validation section, no when-to-revisit, no decision record metadata. Frontmatter lacks vault-path and jira.

B.label: vault_search
B.notes: Vault search provides codebase awareness: references tables.py, memory_forget.py, redactor.py, falkordb_fanout.py, test files, VW-220/VW-422/VW-391. However, mischaracterizes Hamburg DPA as 'January 2024 enforcement action' (was July 2024 discussion paper) -- a factual error likely from vault document ambiguity. Adds 24-month retention and MEMORY_RETENTION_MONTHS (not in ground truth). Has implementation requirements section (useful pragmatic addition). Missing: co-location argument, EDPB CEF 2025, Brandur Leach, Leaky Thoughts arXiv reference, validation section, when-to-revisit.

C.label: vault_search_plus_memory_recall
C.notes: Memory recall provides directional consensus (Fact 1721, 1747) that grounds the decision. Correctly identifies all data layers with schema-level detail including line numbers. All 7 decision controls present and correct. Co-location argument present. Cites EDPB CEF 2025, Brandur Leach, aicompetence.org, Leaky Thoughts with arXiv ID, What Should LLMs Forget, langchain #17499, Mem0. Hamburg DPA correctly dated July 2024. 158 lines -- most concise of all conditions. Missing: explicit validation section and when-to-revisit section (ground truth has both). FalkorDB graph name differs (rootweaver_memory vs document_knowledge).

D.label: vault_plus_memory_plus_prior_art
D.notes: Typed memory evidence (Fact 1721, 1747 with confidence scores) adds provenance. Excellent per-table personal-data classification. Crypto-shredding alternative is a strong addition not in ground truth. However, prior art citations are weaker than expected given condition label: cites 'Hamburg DPA (2023)' (wrong year), references OWASP AI Security and Privacy Guide instead of AISVS C08, references EDPB Guidelines 05/2014 instead of Opinion 28/2024. Missing: EDPB CEF 2025, Brandur Leach, aicompetence.org, Leaky Thoughts, langchain #17499, Mem0, Letta, co-location simplification argument. No validation section, no when-to-revisit.

E.label: vault_plus_memory_plus_prior_art_plus_codebase
E.notes: Near-identical to ground truth. All 7 data layers with schema-level detail and line numbers. All 7 decision controls correct. Co-location argument present with same reasoning (federated vs co-located, Mem0 validation, 7 PKM systems). 14 of 15 ground truth references cited with correct URLs (only missing Milvus). Has validation section (6 SQL checks), when-to-revisit (5 triggers), decision record metadata. Frontmatter matches ground truth exactly (vault-path, jira, related_plan). 217 lines vs ground truth 197 -- additional length is all substantive. The DROP SCHEMA memory CASCADE downgrade path is a codebase-grounded detail not available without code access.

e_vs_c_analysis: E beats C on citation_of_prior_art (+0.10), completeness (+0.10), and pattern_adoption (+0.15). The gains come from codebase grounding: E has the validation section with SQL checks, the when-to-revisit section, decision record metadata, and the DROP SCHEMA CASCADE downgrade path -- all derived from direct code access. E also recovers 3 references C missed (Letta, Crunchy Data, ProvSQL). Both tie on technical_correctness (1.0) and conciseness (1.0). The codebase grounding in E closes the structural completeness gap that C leaves open.

key_findings.d_underperformance: Condition D (prior art) paradoxically scored lower on citation_of_prior_art (0.5) than C (0.85) because it cited the WRONG sources: Hamburg DPA dated 2023 instead of 2024, OWASP AI Privacy Guide instead of AISVS C08, EDPB 05/2014 instead of Opinion 28/2024. Prior art retrieval returned related but non-authoritative documents, and the model trusted them over its own knowledge.
key_findings.c_sweet_spot: C achieves 0.90 composite at presumably lower tool-call cost than E. For ADRs where the codebase doesn't contain validation-ready artifacts, C may be the cost-optimal condition.
key_findings.fabrication_pattern: A and B both fabricate specifics (24-month retention, REST endpoints, January 2024 enforcement action) that are absent from ground truth. Vault search (B) reduced some fabrication but introduced new errors from misinterpreted vault documents.
